articles.title: Live Updates: U.S., Iran reach 2-week ceasefire ahead of Trump's deadline - CBS News
articles.description: President Trump announced that the U.S. and Iran had agreed to a two-week ceasefire that was contingent on the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.description: Trump noted that the "double sided" ceasefire was contingent on Iran agreeing to an opening of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Republican wins runoff to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene in Congress - The Washington Post
articles.description: Clay Fuller, a district attorney backed by President Donald Trump, defeated Shawn Harris in the deeply red district vacated by Marjorie Taylor Greene.

articles.title: Asian benchmarks jump after oil prices sink in response to the Iran ceasefire - AP News
articles.description: Asian shares are surging as oil prices plunged after the U.S. and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire that includes the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Benchmarks rose in early Wednesday trading in Japan, Australia, South Korea and China. Oil prices plunge…

articles.title: The Artemis II Crew Has Sent Back Stunning Images From The Far Side of The Moon - ScienceAlert
articles.description: After a historic lunar flyby that carried four astronauts farther from Earth than humanity has ever been, the Artemis II crew has sent home spectacular photos of the far side of the Moon.

articles.description: The vast majority of rank-and-file Republicans stayed quiet on Tuesday.

articles.title: Telehealth abortion will remain available for now, after a federal judge's ruling - NPR
articles.description: The abortion pill mifepristone must undergo a safety review by the FDA, the judge said. Louisiana's case seeking to ban its use through telemedicine will proceed after that review.

articles.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson released after kidnap in Iraq, officials say - BBC
